summary: The IDF warns that Iran is expected to launch missiles at Israel while Hezbollah rockets have wounded Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon. Israeli forces have intercepted unmanned aircraft from Lebanon and conducted drone strikes in Kfar Tebnit, injuring civilians. Hezbollah has stated its cease‑fire stance is conditional on Israeli compliance, and Israeli strikes have hit multiple towns in southern Lebanon.
key_points: The IDF chief of staff is assessing that Iran will fire missiles at Israel (Source: @manniefabian/58595).
Hezbollah rockets wounded an IDF soldier moderately and another lightly in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58600).
Israeli drones struck a car in Kfar Tebnit, Lebanon, causing injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58602).
The Israeli Air Force intercepted an unmanned aircraft that crossed from Lebanon toward Israeli territory (Source: @idf_telegram/24358).
Several suspected drones struck Israeli territory near the Lebanon border without triggering sirens (Source: @manniefabian/58597).
Israeli artillery and air strikes hit the Lebanese towns of Zawtar, Markaba, and Khiyam (Source: @presstv/194149).
Hezbollah says any cease‑fire is conditional on Israel meeting its demands (Source: @asafroz/50346).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF chief of staff warns that Iran is likely to fire missiles at Israel, prompting heightened alert. Hezbollah rockets have wounded Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon, and Israeli forces have responded with drone strikes and artillery in Lebanese towns. Both sides cite conditional cease‑fire terms, raising the risk of broader conflict.

points: The IDF chief of staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir is conducting an assessment with commanders and currently believes Iran will fire missiles at Israel (Source: @manniefabian/58595).
Hezbollah rockets wounded an IDF soldier moderately and another lightly in southern Lebanon earlier today (Source: @manniefabian/58600).
The Israeli Air Force intercepted an unmanned aircraft that crossed from Lebanon toward Israeli territory, with no civilian alerts issued (Source: @idf_telegram/24358).
Israeli drones struck a car in the southern Lebanese town of Kfar Tebnit, causing injuries, and artillery also hit Kfar Tebnit and nearby Nabatieh al‑Fawqa (Source: @manniefabian/58602).
Israeli forces reported several suspected drones striking Israeli territory near the Lebanon border; no sirens were triggered (Source: @manniefabian/58597).
Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon targeted the towns of Zawtar, Markaba, and Khiyam, according to Lebanese sources (Source: @presstv/194149).

Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir stated that any U.S. agreement does not bind Israel and that Israel will not accept anything less than Hezbollah's disarmament (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3978).

Defense Minister Katz warned that Israel will not withdraw from security zones in Lebanon, Syria, or Gaza without a time limit and cautioned Iran against attacks over Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3981).
Hezbollah rockets wounded Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58600).
Israeli drone strike in Kfar Tebnit injured civilians; artillery also hit Kfar Tebnit and Nabatieh al‑Fawqa (Source: @manniefabian/58602).
Israeli air force intercepted an unmanned aircraft crossing from Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24358).
Israeli strikes hit the Lebanese towns of Zawtar, Markaba, and Khiyam (Source: @presstv/194149).
Hezbollah declared its cease‑fire position is conditional on Israeli actions (Source: @asafroz/50346).

key_developments: IDF chief of staff assesses imminent Iranian missile launch toward Israel, raising regional tension (Source: @manniefabian/58595).
Hezbollah rockets wounded two Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon, indicating active hostilities (Source: @manniefabian/58600).
Israeli Air Force intercepted an unmanned aircraft from Lebanon, showing heightened air defense readiness (Source: @idf_telegram/24358).
Israeli drone strike in Kfar Tebnit caused civilian injuries, demonstrating cross‑border strike capability (Source: @manniefabian/58602).
Multiple suspected drones struck Israeli border areas without triggering civilian alerts, suggesting covert aerial incursions (Source: @manniefabian/58597).
Israeli artillery and air strikes hit Zawtar, Markaba, and Khiyam in southern Lebanon, expanding the conflict zone (Source: @presstv/194149).
Hezbollah conditions any cease‑fire on Israeli compliance, potentially stalling diplomatic resolution (Source: @asafroz/50346).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran appears poised to launch missile attacks against Israel, while Hezbollah continues rocket fire into Israeli positions in southern Lebanon. Israeli retaliatory strikes, including drone and artillery attacks, have targeted multiple Lebanese towns, escalating the front lines. The combination of state and non‑state actors employing missiles, rockets, and unmanned systems raises the risk of a broader regional escalation.
sections.regional_response: Israel has heightened its alert status and reinforced air defenses, while publicly rejecting any external agreements that limit its operational freedom. Hezbollah's conditional cease‑fire stance signals a willingness to continue hostilities unless Israeli demands are met. International actors have not yet issued concrete mediation, leaving the conflict dynamics largely driven by bilateral actions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian missile launch preparations is critical, as is tracking Hezbollah rocket launch sites in southern Lebanon. Continued surveillance of unmanned aerial activity across the Lebanon‑Israel border is required to anticipate further incursions. Israeli strike patterns suggest a focus on disrupting Hezbollah command and control infrastructure, which may provoke retaliatory attacks.
